latest.title: Accounting date, Results & Suspension -Replacement
latest.content:
30 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2651K
Silver Bullet Data Services Grp PLC
30 June 2026
This announcement replaces the announcement with RNS number: 2528K released on 29 June 2026 at 6:18 p.m., with the headline "Change to Accounting Reference Date, Delay in Publication of Annual Results and Suspension". It is noted that the only change that has been made to the announcement has been to update the prospective date of the last day of trading in the Company's Ordinary Shares from 3 July 2026 to 7 July 2026.
30 June 2026
Silver Bullet Data Services Group plc
("
Silverbullet
" or the "
Company
", or, together with its subsidiaries, the "
Group
")
Change to Accounting Reference Date, Delay in Publication of Annual Results and Suspension
Silverbullet (AIM: SBDS), a provider of AI driven digital transformation services and products, provides the following update with respect to its audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2025 ("Annual Results") and announces a suspension from trading on AIM.
On the 26 June 2026, it resolved to change its accounting reference date and financial year end from 31 December to 30 June. This change takes immediate effect.
As a result of this change in the accounting reference date and assuming continued admission to trading on AIM, the last day of trading for which is 7 July 2026 and which is anticipated to be cancelled at 7:00 a.m. (BST) on 8 July 2026, Silverbullet will adhere to the following reporting timetable:
·
publication of its audited results for the 18-month period to 30 June 2026 by 31 December 2026;
·
publication of its unaudited interim financial results for the 6-month period ending 31 December 2026 by 30 June 2027; and
·
publication of its audited results for the 12-month period to 30 June 2027 by 31 December 2027.
As stipulated by Rule 19 of the AIM Rules for Companies (the "AIM Rules"), the Company is required to publish its Annual Results by 30 June 2026. The Company will not to meet this deadline. Accordingly, trading in the Company's ordinary shares on AIM is expected to be suspended with effect from 7.30 a.m. (BST) on 1 July 2026, pending publication of the Annual Results.
For further information please contact:
Silverbullet
Ian James (CEO) / Chris Ellis (CFO)
via IFC
Strand Hanson Limited - Financial and Nominated Adviser
James Spinney / James Bellman / Imogen Ellis
0207 409 3494
Oberon Capital - Broker
Mike Seabrook
020 3179 5300
IFC Advisory
Graham Herring / Tim Metcalfe / Florence Staton
020 3934 663007793 839 024
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR"), and is disclosed in accordance with the company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Result of General Meeting
latest.content:
25 Jun 2026 13:12
RNS Number : 8126J
Silver Bullet Data Services Grp PLC
25 June 2026
25 June 2026
Silver Bullet Data Services Group plc
("
Silverbullet
", the "
Company
", or, together with its subsidiaries, the "
Group
")
Result of General Meeting
Silverbullet (AIM: SBDS), a global provider of data and technology services, announces that, at the general meeting of the Company held earlier today (the "
General Meeting
"), the special resolutions to approve the canellation of the admission of the Company's Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM, and re-register as a private company and approve the adoption of new articles of association (the "
Resolutions
"), were put to the Company's shareholders ("
Shareholders
") and were duly passed.
The full text of the Resolutions proposed and passed at the General Meeting can be found in the Circular containing
, inter alia
, the Notice of General Meeting, which was published at 7.00 a.m. on 9 June 2026 and is available on the Company's website
https://investors.wearesilverbullet.com
.
The total votes cast on the Resolutions, incorporating proxy votes lodged in advance of the General Meeting, were as set out below:
Resolution Number
Resolution Name
Votes For
Votes Against
No. of shares
% of shares voted
No. of shares
% of shares voted
1
Cancellation of admission of Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM
7,560,215
92.95%
573,403
7.05%
2
Re-registration and change of Articles of Association
7,560,215
92.95%
573,403
7.05%
AIM Cancellation
In accordance with Rule 41 of the AIM Rules for Companies, cancellation of the admission fo the Company's Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM is expected to become effective at 7:00 a.m. on Wednesday, 8 July 2026 and, accordingly, the last day of dealings in Ordinary Shares on AIM is expected to be Tuesday, 7 July 2026. Any amendments to this timetable will be notified to shareholders.
Re-registration as a private company
In accordance with the passing of Resolution 2, the Company will re-register as a private limited company and adopt new articles of association, which is expected to take place week commencing 27 July 2026.
Matched Bargain Facility
To facilitate future shareholder transactions in the Company's Ordinary Shares, the Company has appointed JP Jenkins to establish a share Matched Bargain Facility in order to assist shareholders wishing to trade in the Company's Ordinary Shares post the AIM Cancellation. While the exact duration may vary, the Board currently expects it to be available for at least one year following Cancellation. Upon Cancellation, full details of the Matched Bargain Facility will be made available to Shareholders on the Company's website
https://investors.wearesilverbullet.com
.
Capitalised terms used but not defined in this announcement shall have the same meaning given to such terms in the Circular.
For further information please
contact:
Silver Bullet Data Services Group PLC
Ian James (CEO) / Chris Ellis (CFO)
via IFC
Strand Hanson Limited
- Financial and Nominated Adviser
James Spinney / James Bellman / Imogen Ellis
+44 (0)207 409 3494
Oberon Capital - Broker
Mike Seabrook
+44 (0) 203 179 5300
IFC Advisory
Graham Herring / Tim Metcalfe
+44 (0) 203 934 6633
About Silverbullet
Silverbullet's proprietary 4D AI advertising solution is designed to help advertisers target consumers in a "privacy-first world". The product is a natural extension to its existing services business which already serves a blue-chip client base such as a leading UK hospitality brand and a Global Brewing company, amongst many others.
Headquartered in London, the Group employs more than 85 data specialists across five regions across the globe, including, the UK, Italy, Australia, USA and Latin America. The Group continues to look at other opportunities for expansion worldwide.
The Company has an established and growing solutions business with significant accumulated industry experience and a proven track record of delivering strategic digital transformation and activation services to its clients. Most of the Board have held senior positions at global software companies and have significant industry experience across data engineering, SaaS product development and marketing.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: SBDS appears to be a small defence‑related company that supplies equipment or services to the UK Ministry of Defence.  It has been involved in a few MOD contracts and has been discussing funding and restructuring with investors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Defence spending is a large, stable part of the UK economy.  Companies that can secure MOD contracts often enjoy long‑term revenue streams, especially when the government renews or expands its procurement programmes.  SBDS’s potential to win or renew contracts could give it a foothold in a sector with limited competition.
company_30s.current_state: The company is currently trading very low and is in the midst of a major restructuring.  Recent filings show a proposed AIM delisting and an AGM that could decide its future.  It is likely in a cash‑burn phase, looking for fresh capital or a strategic partnership.

rns_narrative: SBDS is facing a critical decision point: the company has announced a proposed delisting from AIM and is holding a general meeting to decide whether to stay listed or restructure.  The board is also looking for new funding and has been in talks about equity issuances and a wrapped retail offer.

There is a clear theme of survival and repositioning.  The company is trying to keep the market alive by securing new capital, potentially through a private placement or a larger investment round.  If the AGM votes to stay listed and the funding comes through, the company could stabilize and start generating revenue again.

The narrative also shows a shift from operational uncertainty to a focus on capital structure.  If the company can secure the needed cash, it could pivot to a more defensible business model and start delivering value to shareholders.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent but tense – the community is waiting for a decisive move.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders are hopeful that a successful equity raise or a new MOD contract will lift the stock.  They see the deep crash as a buying opportunity if the company can secure fresh capital.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many are worried that the proposed delisting will be approved, leading to a total loss of value.  The lack of recent positive news and the potential for continued cash burn are key fears.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has moved from neutral chatter to cautious optimism among the few bullish voices.  The overall mood is still wary, but the absence of widespread negative sentiment gives room for a surprise turnaround.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent interest, indicating the market is largely unaware of SBDS.  This silence means any new announcement could create a big surprise.

near_term_catalysts: A decision at the AGM that the company will remain listed and will raise at least £1 million in equity or convertible debt.
The approval of a new MOD contract or extension of an existing one, which would provide a clear revenue stream.
An announcement of a partnership or acquisition that brings in new expertise or capital.
A significant increase in trading volume that breaks the current low‑volume plateau, signalling renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in daily volume above 5× the current average, a clear statement from the board about a successful capital raise, or a press release confirming a new MOD contract.  If any of these happen, it would confirm that the crash is over and a rebound is likely.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of capitulation – the price has fallen steeply and trading has thinned.  If the APEX score were high, it would suggest the market is still holding a tight squeeze, ready to release once a catalyst appears.  The key is to wait for the AGM or a funding announcement before committing.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down more than 80 % from its peak, which is the sweet spot for a deep‑crash play.  With no active sentiment and dead search interest, any positive news is likely to trigger a sharp rebound.  If the price recovers to the midpoint between its current level and the all‑time high, that’s roughly a 200 % upside – a classic asymmetric reward.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company is still fighting to stay listed and may not raise the capital it needs.  A vote to delist would wipe out the share value.  Even if the stock stays listed, continued cash burn could force another sale or liquidation, limiting upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~200 % if the price climbs to the midpoint of the crash.  Potential downside: essentially 0 % because the share is already at the bottom of its range.  The risk‑reward ratio is therefore about 200:1, highly favourable for a contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the AGM if the company confirms a capital raise or a new MOD contract.  Use a small position (e.g., 5–10 % of available capital) and set a stop 10 % below the entry to protect against a sudden reversal.  Target the midpoint of the crash for a partial exit, and hold the remainder for a longer‑term play.
playbook.if_watching: Watch for volume spikes of 5× the average and any press release about funding or contracts.  If the AGM results in a delisting vote, exit immediately.  If the AGM is neutral, keep an eye on the next quarterly update for signs of new capital.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a delisting or a bankruptcy filing, consider cutting the position to avoid a total loss.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SBDS is a deep‑crashed, sentiment‑dead defence‑supplier that could rebound dramatically if it secures new capital or a MOD contract – a classic high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play.
